programmes.show_description: H from Steps has your ultimate party playlist. Not just for Fridays!
See programme schedules for BBC Radio 5 Live.
Everything you need to know as you wake up.
Your dedications and song requests from the 1950s-90s. Can you guess the Stuck in the Middle song? Text 81012, email moneyfornothing@bbc.co.uk or call 03700 100 110.
Fire chef Chris Flamebaster Roberts chats to Lucy about his new Flaming Feasts series on BBC1 Wales, and comedian Jack Whitehall talks about his new UK tour, Bad Influence.
Live commentary, guests and analysis. Your home for Welsh sport.
Huw Stephens presents two hours of brilliant live music as he delves into the BBC Archive for rare versions of some of Wales greatest songs, both onstage and in session.
Bethan shares live music and a chat with Al Morrison at Neuadd Ogwen in Bethesda, and speaks to event organiser Dilwyn Llywd. Plus brilliant new releases in The Wales Mix.
Adam catches up with Mali Hâf to discuss her new Miwsli EP. We dive into the vault to uncover an interview with Kristin Hersh. Plus the best in new Welsh music.
Relax, unwind and chill out with an hour of classic and contemporary tunes.
See programme schedules for BBC Radio 5 Live.
This programme was first broadcast in 2025. Gary Raymond is in conversation with Ceri Dupree.

programmes.show_name: We Can't Go On Eating Like This!
programmes.show_description: Are there ways in which we can regain power and control over what we eat? And how realistic are the alternatives?
Ifan Jones Evans is at home on the farm in Ceredigion, and experiences the ups and downs of lambing in the hills.
The Reverend Deb Stammers, of New Park Street Baptist Church in Holyhead, leads a special Palm Sunday service.
Nick Servini with political news, discussions and analysis.
Jonathan Thomas considers what's involved in organising this year's Royal Maundy Service at St Asaph Cathedral in north Wales.
A second dose of Owen Money for the weekend. It's nonstop music all the way, with classics from the 60s, 70s and 80s, plus your dedications!
Robin brings you the best in music and guests for your Sunday afternoon. Call 03700 100 110 Text 81012 or message us via X @bbcradiowales.
Music, entertainment and great guests for your Sunday afternoon.
Roy is joined by Gethin Russell Jones and discovers more about Easter traditions both here in Wales and elsewhere. Roy also meets Cardiff singer and musician Molly Roberts.
...Wales's Redcoats in the American Revolution. In the year of America's 250th anniversary, we tell the story of the side that lost.
The Reverend Deb Stammers, of New Park Street Baptist Church in Holyhead, leads a special Palm Sunday service.
Frank Hennessy presents the best in folk, roots and acoustic music from the Celtic countries and beyond.
The finest musical melodies, exquisite performances from stage and screen, and orchestral and choral works from Wales and the world. There's no need to book your seat, just listen and relax.
